description: A taxonomy of rhetorical tactics used for polarization and deflection, particularly in political discourse.

tactics.name: Us vs. Them Framing
tactics.description: Casting the discourse as a conflict between two monolithic, opposing groups, often with moral judgment attached.

tactics.name: Demonization
tactics.description: Portraying opponents as evil, inhuman, or a threat to the nation or way of life.

tactics.name: Emotional Appeal
tactics.description: Using emotionally charged language to bypass rational argument and provoke fear, anger, or pity.

tactics.name: Sovereignty Shield
tactics.description: Invoking national sovereignty, independence, or pride to deflect from or reject external accountability, laws, or criticism.

tactics.name: Religious/Divine Mandate
tactics.description: Claiming actions are sanctioned by God or a higher power to grant them unquestionable moral authority and deflect criticism.

tactics.name: Whataboutism
tactics.description: Dismissing criticism by pointing to the real or perceived flaws of the accuser or other parties.

tactics.name: Challenge to Legal Process
tactics.description: Questioning the legitimacy of legal institutions or processes when facing investigation or accountability.
